findings.evidence: `Diagram.lean:264` defines `GridState.swapColumns`, `Diagram.lean:287` already has the generic point-set transport lemma for swaps, while `RectangleSwap.lean:99`, `147`, `171`, and `202` repeat this bookkeeping only under the stronger rectangle hypothesis.
findings.fix: First add general lemmas in the `GridState` swap-column namespace, e.g. for `h : a ≠ b`: `(p ∈ x.pointSet ∩ (x.swapColumns a b).pointSet ↔ p ∈ x.pointSet ∧ p.1 ≠ a ∧ p.1 ≠ b)`, the two insert decompositions with `(a, x a)` and `(b, x b)`, and the cardinality `= n - 2`; then derive the rectangle statements using `R.target_eq_swapColumns` and `bottom`/`top`.
findings.issue: `mem_inter_pointSet_iff`, `source_pointSet_eq`, `target_pointSet_eq`, and `card_inter_pointSet` require a `GridRectangleBetween x y`, but these are plain consequences of swapping two distinct columns of a grid state.

summary: The rectangle-to-column-swap identification is at the right level, but the point-set bookkeeping is stated only for rectangles even though it is naturally a `GridState.swapColumns` API.
